docs.title: Welcome to jimmy's documentation!
docs.text: jimmy is a tool to import your notes from different formats to Joplin.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Download jimmy here: Linux | Windows | MacOS

Note

The MacOS app is untested.

Alternative installation options:

  1. CLI app: jimmy-cli-*. Available at the release page.
  2. Clone the repository and use it from python by python src/jimmy_cli.py.
  3. GUI app (experimental). Can be used from python python src/jimmy_gui.py or built manually by python -m PyInstaller jimmy_gui.spec.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
1. This script requires that the webclipper in Joplin is running. It will connect to Joplin at the first execution.
  2. Import from text files or import from specific apps, like Google Keep
  3. Verify that everything was imported properly. The imported notes should be available in a new Joplin notebook named like YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - Import.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
• General discussion at the Joplin forum
  • Report a bug or request a new feature at github

docs.title: How to add a new format?
docs.text:
Here is a good example commit.

The brief workflow is:

  1. Discuss the format at the forum or github
  2. Implement at src/
  3. Provide an example file and add a test
  4. Lint with lint.sh
  5. Document at docs/

docs.title: Why Joplin's data API is used?
docs.text:
• Plain markdown: Tags aren't supported.
  • JEX: Requires to work with some internals that I would rather not touch.
  • Joplin's data API: Straight forward to use and supports all needed functions.

docs.title: Why enlighten and not tqdm for progress bars?
docs.text: enlighten did integrate easier with pythons logging.

docs.title: Import structure
docs.text:
• Cacher labels are converted to Joplin tags.
  • Cacher snippets are converted to Joplin notebooks.
  • Cacher files are converted to Joplin notes.

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Only markdown files are converted for now.
  • Files attached to snippets are not exported.

docs.title: Export instructions
docs.text:
• https://www.giuspen.com/cherrytreemanual/#_exporting
  • Choose "Export" -> "Export to CherryTreeDocument" -> "All the Tree" -> "XML, Not Protected (.cdt)"

docs.title: Import structure
docs.text:
• CherryTree nodes are converted to Joplin notes.
  • If a CherryTree node contains sub nodes, a Joplin Notebook is created as container. It has the same name as the corresponding note.

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Cherrytree checked (☑) and crossed checkboxes (☒) are converted to markdown checked checkboxes ([x]).
  • Cherrytree latex is converted to a markdown code block.
  • Some cherrytree elements are not inserted at their original location, but at the end of the note. This is a limitation of cherrytree's export.
  • Cherrytree anchors are not converted.

docs.title: Export instructions
docs.text:
Note

Export works with the mobile app only. See https://github.com/clipto-pro/Desktop/issues/21#issuecomment-537401330.

docs.title: Default Import
docs.text: The default import can be used to import single files or folders recursively.

docs.title: Export instructions
docs.text:
The default import covers the following formats:

  • Every format that is supported by pandoc. Some formats may need some tweaking, though. A few examples:
    • CSV
    • DocBook
    • docx
    • EPUB
    • HTML
    • Jupyter notebook
    • ODT
    • OPML
    • MediaWiki
    • reStructuredText
    • RTF
    • txt2tags
  • asciidoc (requires asciidoctor installed and in path)
  • Fountain (has a built-in Joplin plugin that needs to be activated in the settings)
  • Markdown (Commonmark)
  • txt

docs.title: Import to Joplin
docs.text:
Examples:

# import a single file supported by pandoc
jimmy-cli-linux libre_office_document.odt

# import multiple files
jimmy-cli-linux plaintext.txt markdown.md

# import all files in a folder recursively
jimmy-cli-linux path/to/folder

docs.title: Import structure
docs.text:
• Folders are converted to Joplin notebooks
  • Files are converted to Joplin notes

docs.title: FuseBase
docs.text:
Also known as Nimbus Note.

  • Website
  • Typical extension: Folder with .zip files

docs.title: Export instructions
docs.text:
• https://jrnl.sh/en/stable/formats/#exporting-with-file
  • Export as json to preserve metadata.
  • Example: jrnl --format json --file myjournal.json

docs.title: Import structure
docs.text: Each journal entry is converted to a separate note in a flat notebook.

docs.title: Export instructions
docs.text:
• https://www.notion.so/de-de/help/export-your-content
  • Choose "Markdown and CSV" and uncheck "Create folder for sub-pages" when exporting.

docs.title: Import to Joplin
docs.text: Example: jimmy-cli-linux "Standard Notes Backup - Sun Apr 28 2024 12_56_55 GMT+0200.zip" --format standard_notes

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note links, attachments and folders are not implemented yet, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: Note content is imported in TiddlyWiki's WikiText format and not converted to markdown.

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text:
• Finished todo's are not exported at all.
  • Subtasks are converted to regular notes. I. e. they lose their indentation.
  • Markdown is not rendered in note titles.

docs.title: Known limitations
docs.text:
• Note links are not exported.
  • Multiple formats are not converted properly.

docs.title: Import to Joplin
docs.text:
Examples:

  • jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_completed_240427.csv --format toodledo
  • jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_current_240427.csv --format toodledo
  • jimmy-cli-linux toodledo_notebook_240427.csv --format toodledo

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: subtasks and files are not implemented, since they require a subscription.

docs.title: Import structure
docs.text:
• xit groups are converted to Joplin notebooks
  • xit items are converted to Joplin todos
  • Priority, tags and due dates are converted to metadata and removed from the todo title

docs.title: Known Limitations
docs.text: • Checklists are converted to plain lists. This might change with a newer pandoc version.
